channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government doctors denied permission for hunger strike over pay demands
item.description: The Legal Coordination Committee (LCC) representing government doctors has strongly criticised the authorities for denying permission to conduct a hunger strike at the Rajiv Gandhi Government General ...

item.title: Gateway Airport to Boost Cargo Movement Across Nigeria and West Africa
item.description: The Nigeria Shippers’ Council CEO, Akutah Pius Okeyima, don talk say the Gateway International Airport na very important infrastructure wey fit solve the wahala wey dey cargo movement for Nigeria and ...

item.title: Sault man penalized $3,000 for leaving ice fishing hut unattended
item.description: A 68-year-old man from Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario, has been fined $3,000 for abandoning his ice fishing hut on a local lake, violating regional regulations intended to protect the environment and ensur...

item.title: Iran’s World Cup participation in doubt as US‑Iran conflict rocks FIFA plans
item.description: The article explains how the escalating military conflict between the United States and Iran is casting serious doubt over Team Melli’s participation in the 2026 FIFA World Cup in the United States, C...

item.title: Parañaque condo shooting suspect linked to previous homicide case, says NCRPO
item.description: Authorities in Parañaque have confirmed that the suspect involved in a recent condominium shooting has a prior homicide conviction. The incident has prompted local law enforcement to review the suspec...

item.title: Ongoing Tensions and Developments in the Middle East Conflict
item.description: As of March 8, 2026, the situation in the Middle East remains tense, with ongoing conflicts involving key nations such as the United States and Iran. The news coverage provides continuous updates on m...

item.title: TV Highlights: Scarpetta, Vladimir, Home And Away, and Harry Styles Live
item.description: This week, Screen Queen Clare Rigden dives into a variety of TV treats. On Netflix, 'Vladimir' stars Rachel Weisz as a university lecturer whose midlife crisis leads to an unexpected obsession with a ...

item.title: Kotane Lekota appointed interim curator following father’s mental incapacity
item.description: The Gauteng Division of the High Court has declared that veteran politician Mosiuoa 'Terror' Lekota, who passed away at 77, was mentally incapacitated and unable to manage his affairs from 25 May 2025...

item.title: Morrisons upgrades checkouts and accessible toilets nationwide
item.description: Morrisons is implementing a major upgrade across all its stores to improve the efficiency of both self-service and colleague-operated checkout machines. The nationwide refresh, which began in May 2025...

item.title: Remembering Papa Zeal: A Life of Friendship and Generosity
item.description: Bongo has changed plenty since the old days when a beer cost just Sh2.50. People born after the 1990s might not believe there was a time when beer was scarce and enjoyed mostly at month-end after civi...

item.title: Kristi Noem Removed from Position, Markwayne Mullin Appointed
item.description: In a recent development in U.S. political circles, Kristi Noem, previously nicknamed 'ICE Queen' and 'ICE Barbie,' has been removed from her position, reportedly by order of former President Donald Tr...

item.title: Health Ministry Advises Kenyans on Disease Prevention and Safety Amid Floods
item.description: The Ministry of Health has issued a public advisory following heavy floods across Kenya. Citizens have been warned about increased risks of waterborne and vector-borne diseases such as cholera, typhoi...

item.title: Alex Albon on balancing Formula 1 racing with a private life
item.description: Alex Albon, the British-Thai Formula 1 driver, has chosen a quieter path in an era where the sport has become as much about celebrity as racing. Competing for Williams Racing, Albon avoids social medi...

item.title: Court of Appeal Grants Interim Stay Restoring JSC's Complaint Processing Role
item.description: The Court of Appeal has issued an interim stay, suspending a High Court decision that had blocked the Judicial Service Commission (JSC) from handling complaints against judges and judicial officers. T...

item.title: The Pitt TV Series Wins Award, Highlights Contributions of Filipino Nurses in US Healthcare
item.description: The HBO Max medical drama 'The Pitt,' set in a high-pressure Pittsburgh emergency room, recently bagged the Best Ensemble in a Drama Series at the 2026 SAG-AFTRA Awards. This victory puts the spotligh...

item.title: South African Fuel Prices Set to Increase in April Amid Global Oil Price Rise
item.description: South African drivers are likely to face significant fuel price hikes in April 2026, with increases potentially reaching up to R8 per litre. This surge follows a sharp jump in the rand price of Brent ...

item.title: Saudi Arabia Steps Up Diplomatic Talks with Iran Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Saudi Arabia has accelerated its diplomatic engagement with Iran in an effort to prevent the escalation of conflict in the Middle East, which has already affected global markets. Using back-channel co...

item.title: Six Spanish Female Directors Promoting Linguistic Diversity Through Cinema
item.description: Spain's film industry, historically dominated by male directors and the Spanish language, is witnessing a shift thanks to pioneering female filmmakers and supportive organisations like CIMA (the Assoc...

item.title: The Risks of U.S. Support for Iranian Kurdish Opposition Forces
item.description: As the Iranian regime faces significant pressure from U.S. and Israeli military action, Washington has reportedly explored the idea of supporting Kurdish opposition groups in Iran to destabilize the c...

item.title: Spain Partially Evacuates Personnel from Tehran Embassy Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: In response to escalating tensions in the Iran conflict during early March 2026, Spain has taken measures to ensure the safety of its diplomatic personnel in Tehran. The Wall Street Journal live cover...
